blockingIssues.description: Tests use extremely weak assertions (mostly `bodyText?.trim().length > 0`) that verify nothing about error handling behavior. VAL-ERROR-001 promises 'user-friendly error message' but tests don't verify any specific message.
Tests use `.catch(() => {})` which silently swallows assertion failures. Tests can pass even if error toasts never appear.
Timeout tests don't actually verify timeout behavior. Uses 30-second delayed response but only 5-second wait, so timeout is never triggered.
Test 'Error boundary catches React errors' doesn't actually trigger any error - page.evaluate just adds an event listener without triggering it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add section documenting Toast CSS class naming convention (.toast--success, .toast--error, .toast-container, .toast__title, .toast__message, .toast__close) and known toast trigger points with expected messages.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: e2e-toast-notifications-comprehensive review found these are used in tests but not documented. Workers need to know exact selectors and trigger points.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that tests must verify specific UI elements appear, not just that 'body has content'. Add examples of proper error message assertions vs weak 'length > 0' assertions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: e2e-error-handling-comprehensive ignored AGENTS.md assertion patterns entirely, using meaningless assertions throughout.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on testing toast notifications, including common selectors, the 5-second auto-dismiss behavior for timing, and proper patterns for verifying toast content.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: e2e-worker skill lacks toast-specific guidance. e2e-toast-notifications-comprehensive derived tests from reading source code rather than skill guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the 300ms wait after viewport changes for React to re-render as a convention for responsive testing.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: e2e-responsive-design-comprehensive uses this pattern but it's not documented for other workers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that helper functions should be imported from 'e2e/test-db-helpers' (not 'e2e/helpers.ts' as the skill document incorrectly states).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Skill documentation says 'e2e/helpers.ts' but actual project uses 'e2e/test-db-helpers.ts'. Naming inconsistency causes confusion.

rejectedObservations.observation: Toast CSS class naming should go in library
rejectedObservations.reason: AGENTS.md is the appropriate place for CSS class conventions that workers need to know

rejectedObservations.observation: VAL-RESP assertion criteria detailed in validation-contract.md
rejectedObservations.reason: validation-contract.md is owned by orchestrator; library is not the place to suggest changes to it
